dataset: WASP-12b Transit Observations from University of Louisville

pi_items.source: install_astroimagej.sh script downloads from https://www.astro.louisville.edu/software/astroimagej/examples/; directory listing confirms WASP-12b calibrated images available
Verified to exist and be accessible; file size 4.3GB as documented in install_astroimagej.sh; confirmed via web search of University of Louisville AstroImageJ examples directory
WASP-12 is a well-documented exoplanet host star with discovery in 2008; NASA Exoplanet Archive contains full characterization; host of the hot Jupiter WASP-12b with transit depth 1.38±0.02%
Stated in README.md: '186 calibrated r-band CCD frames'; stated in setup_task.sh ground truth and setup output; consistent across all documentation
README.md states '186 calibrated r-band CCD frames of the WASP-12 stellar field'; setup_task.sh reads FITS header FILTER field to verify filter on actual data

NASA Exoplanet Archive lists WASP-12b transit depth as 1.38±0.02%. Published literature (Croll et al. 2013 MNRAS 434, Mandell et al. 2013) report consistent transit depths; 1.4% is the rounded value within measurement uncertainty
No external authoritative source for tolerance value; appears to be task design parameter chosen for scoring rubric (verifier.py line 35)
No external authoritative source; appears to be task design requirement (verifier.py line 36); reasonable for differential photometry (industry standard requires at least 1-2 comparison stars)

pi_items.metadata_value: MORC24 telescope (Moore Observatory, University of Louisville)
pi_items.verified_value: MORC24 is a 0.6m (24-inch) Ritchey-Chretien telescope at Moore Observatory, University of Louisville
pi_items.source: Web search confirms Moore Observatory RC optical telescope (MORC) 0.6m/24-inch manufactured by RC Optical Systems, installed August 2006, currently used to measure transiting exoplanets including WASP-12b

privileged_info_summary: The task uses 186 real calibrated r-band FITS frames of the WASP-12 field from the University of Louisville MORC24 (24-inch Ritchey-Chretien) telescope, available at https://www.astro.louisville.edu/software/astroimagej/examples/WASP-12b_example_calibrated_images.tar.gz (4.3 GB). WASP-12 is the host star of the exoplanet WASP-12b, which exhibits a transit depth of approximately 1.38±0.02% (task specifies 1.4%). The data is entirely real astronomical observations, not synthetic. The agent must identify WASP-12 as the variable star through multi-aperture differential photometry and characterize the transit depth and timing.
